steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: An effective introduction must convey both who Fessenden was and what he is known for: pioneering radio technology that led to the first transmission of speech by radio.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — role and workplace only: Identifies Fessenden as an electronic communications specialist who worked for Edison, but omits his defining achievement — the radio transmission breakthrough. ✗
  • Choice B — background and achievement: Names his background (electronic communications specialist, Edison connection) and his achievement (pioneering radio technology that led to the first transmission of speech by radio). Both required elements are present. ✓
  • Choice C — Edison-focused: Shifts focus to Edison and Sprague rather than Fessenden's own contribution. ✗
  • Choice D — Edison-focused: Also centers Edison and Sprague, leaving Fessenden's radio achievement unmentioned. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
